own_review.point: gluonhq/setup-graalvm pinned to @master with default graalvm:latest — mutable ref, non-reproducible builds and supply-chain risk; pin to a tag/SHA and explicit GraalVM version
GUI jobs lose the maven dependency cache the old graalvm/setup-graalvm step provided (cache: 'maven'); Gluon action has no cache input — slower CI for all GUI matrix legs

JDK 23 is non-LTS and at/near EOL at PR time (JDK 24 GA Mar 2025): actions/setup-java distribution 'oracle' + java-version 23 (codeql.yml, formatter.yml) and download.oracle.com/graalvm/23/latest (Docker dev base) will stop resolving once Oracle drops 23 from 'latest'; LTS path (21->25) safer

dev Docker environment (docker-compose -> Docker/dev/commons/base) still installs plain Oracle GraalVM 23 for GUI dev builds while CI switched GUI to Gluon's GraalVM — the gluonfx build problem the PR fixes in CI presumably persists in the dev container

new reachability-metadata.json added while the old split configs (reflect-config.json, jni-config.json, serialization-config.json, etc.) remain in the same META-INF/native-image dir — duplication/possible conflicts; old files should be removed or reconciled

resource-config.json rewritten as a single minified line — unreviewable/undiffable; content also silently changed: root locale "" dropped from bundles, SplashLight.png missing from the new globs section (only SplashDark present)

Windows GUI profile pins gluonfx back to 1.0.23 (1.0.25 unsupported on Windows per the added comment) while CI now installs Gluon GraalVM java23 — old plugin + new JDK combination untested; author admits only Ubuntu verified

inconsistent arch values across workflows: 'x86_64' in build.yml vs 'amd64' in docker-publish.yml; the gluon action only documents x86_64/aarch64 ('amd64' happens to work because non-aarch64 falls through to amd64 for java23)
parent pom still depends on JavaFX 23-ea+20 (early access) — upgrading the JDK to 23 while staying on EA JavaFX is inconsistent; move to stable JavaFX 23.x
maven.compiler source/target duplicated in 4 poms plus compiler-plugin config — PR had to touch 4 places; centralize (e.g. maven.compiler.release in parent only)

qualitative: All four candidates were hurt by the truncated diff and hallucinated to different degrees, but v2 is clearly the best: it is the only one to flag the unpinned gluonhq/setup-graalvm@master ref (a genuine, verified issue), it caught the EA JavaFX dependency, and it hedged on the parent-pom question instead of asserting a false 'critical' — though it fabricated release dates for native-build-tools and a wrong ICU-76 claim, and incorrectly said docker-compose wasn't updated. v1 and v3 both invented the same false blocker (root pom/compiler-plugin still at 21 — the actual diff updates both) and otherwise padded with praise; each salvaged only the JavaFX-EA point. v4 is the worst: three confidently-asserted fabricated criticals, including an invented empty <version> tag (an artifact of the diff truncation point) and made-up plugin release dates, with no verified positive finding. Nobody made the human reviewer's point that the explicit 'amd64' arch in docker-publish is unnecessary, and all missed the lost maven cache for GUI jobs, the leftover old native-image configs / silently rewritten resource-config (dropped SplashLight glob), the untested Windows gluonfx-1.0.23+java23 combo, and the non-LTS JDK 23 EOL fragility of the oracle setup-java and download URLs.
